name: Pamela's NEW Workout
description: Programmable clocked modulation source with 8 independent outputs. Each output freely configurable as gate, triangle, sine, envelope, or random, with Euclidean patterns, random skipping, quantization and inter-output logic. BPM 10-300.

controls.name: Program Knob
controls.description: Push-turn encoder. Twist scrolls outputs/values, click selects, long-hold (>1s) enters extended parameter menu per output.
controls.detail: Push: select · Twist: adjust · Hold: extended params · blue illuminated

controls.name: Start / Stop Button
controls.description: Starts and stops the internal clock. Stop also resets clock position.

inputs.name: Clk
inputs.description: External clock input. Syncs internal clock at 1-48 PPQN (24 PPQN DIN Sync default).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.description: Gate-level run/stop input. Assignable alternatives: Reset, Load Sequenced Bank, Rotate Outputs, Load CV Bank.

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: Unipolar CV input. Assignable to any output parameter with per-target attenuation and offset. Ignored outside range.

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: Bipolar CV input. Assignable to any output parameter with per-target attenuation and offset. 0V = 50%.

outputs.name: OUT 1-8
outputs.description: Eight independent clocked CV/gate outputs with green level LEDs. Each fully programmable.

behaviors.name: Clock divisions and multipliers
behaviors.description: Each output can be set to a multiplier or divisor of the master clock from /512 to x48, including non-integer triplet and dotted values. Four utility modifiers replace the rate: constant ON (gate high), OFF, single start pulse, and single stop pulse — useful for resetting external sequencers.

behaviors.name: Waveform shapes
behaviors.description: Six base waveforms per step: Gate, Triangle, Sine, Envelope, Random, Smooth Random. A full cycle fits one step. Width parameter reshapes each: gate pulse-width, triangle/sine skew towards saw, envelope release length, smooth random peak roundness. Random does not respond to Width.

behaviors.name: Complex swing via Delay + Dly
behaviors.description: Delay shifts the start of waveform generation by a percentage of step time; Dly specifies which steps are delayed (every step, every other, every third). Dly=2 produces classic swing. Combined with Phase offset this covers most common groove templates without external MIDI-style swing.

behaviors.name: Euclidean patterns
behaviors.description: EStep sets total steps, ETrig sets the number of hits distributed as equidistantly as possible. ERot rotates the resulting pattern by N steps. Non-hit steps are skipped entirely — no waveform fires. Works on any waveform type, not just gates.

behaviors.name: Random skip and Loop
behaviors.description: RSkip is a percentage chance each step is skipped. Loop specifies a number of beats after which the random sequence resets — re-seeds from the saved seed, so loops are recallable after power cycle. Setting Loop=0 re-seeds the RNG for a fresh pattern.

behaviors.name: Quantizer (10-bit, 5 octaves)
behaviors.description: Quantises the final output voltage to a selected scale (Major, Minor, Harmonic Minor, Pentatonic Major/Minor, Lydian, Phrygian, Japanese, root+fifth, I/IV/VI chords, 12-tone chromatic). Applied after all other processing. When quantised, random-waveform outputs are S&H across empty euclidean/skipped steps instead of dropping to 0V, producing more musical sequences.

behaviors.name: Inter-output logic (AND / OR / XOR)
behaviors.description: Each output can combine its raw signal with any one other output via AND, OR, or XOR. Enables rhythm subdivision, rhythm thinning, or rhythm expansion from a single device — e.g. OUT 1 x4 AND OUT 2 /3 fires only on coincident beats.

behaviors.name: Slop (timing humanisation)
behaviors.description: Slop introduces a percentage chance of small random timing offsets per step. Humanises rigid clock patterns — classic drunk-drummer feel on hi-hats, or light 5-10% slop for subtle natural groove.

behaviors.name: CV assignment with per-target atten/offset
behaviors.description: Any parameter except Steps can be assigned to CV 1 or CV 2. Each assignment has its own software attenuation (signed, acts as attenuverter) and offset. Multiple parameters can follow the same CV input with different scaling.

behaviors.name: Run input assignable modes
behaviors.description: Beyond start/stop, Run can trigger: Reset (resets all outputs to initial state), Load Sequenced Bank (advances through saved banks — direction set by CV 1 level), Rotate Outputs (shifts pattern assignment across outputs, direction by CV 1), Load CV Bank (CV 1 voltage selects bank A-P).

behaviors.name: Save banks (a-z × 8 slots)
behaviors.description: 26 banks each holding 8 output configurations, plus all-output group save/load. Seeds for random waveforms are saved — so a loop from 6 months ago replays identically. Saving while clock is running may introduce small timing glitches.

behaviors.name: Mute toggle shortcut
behaviors.description: Holding the encoder while tapping Start/Stop toggles the current output's mute state without scrolling into a menu — live performance feature for dropping voices in and out of a patch.

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean drum bed
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 1 E(3,16) → kick · OUT 2 E(5,16) ERot=2 → snare · OUT 3 E(7,16) ERot=5 → hat · three primes produce a 16-step groove that never loops identically within a phrase · classic Euclidean rhythm generator
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate x1, EStep=16 ETrig=3
* OUT 2: Gate x1, EStep=16 ETrig=5 ERot=2
* OUT 3: Gate x1, EStep=16 ETrig=7 ERot=5

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 3) t> Hat (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Quantised random melody
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 1 Random wave · /4 · Quant=Pentatonic Minor · Level=100% → VCO V/oct · OUT 2 Gate /4 → envelope · new pitch on every beat, always in scale · loop=8 creates a recallable 8-beat melody
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 1: Random wave, /4, Quant=Pm, Loop=8
* OUT 2: Gate /4

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/oct) // scale-locked random melody
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 2) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Logic AND for ghost notes
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 1 gate /16 (dense) · OUT 2 E(5,16) (sparse pattern) · OUT 1 Logic=AND channel 2 · OUT 1 now fires only where both are high · syncopated ghost hits filtered from a straight 16th pulse
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /16, Logic=AND ch2
* OUT 2: Gate x1, EStep=16 ETrig=5

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Perc (Trig) // dense AND sparse = ghost hits

patchIdeas.name: Sine quadrature stereo
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 5 Sine /1 bar Phase=0 → Pan L CV · OUT 6 Sine /1 bar Phase=25 (90°) → Pan R CV · 90° offset creates circular stereo motion · tempo-synced without external LFO
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 5: Sine /1, Phase=0
* OUT 6: Sine /1, Phase=25

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 5) >> VCA L (CV)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 6) >> VCA R (CV) // quadrature pan

patchIdeas.name: DIN Sync tempo master
patchIdeas.patch: External DIN Sync device → Clk (24 PPQN) · Run input tracks external start/stop · Pam syncs tightly as slave while still generating all 8 clocked outputs · bridges legacy drum machines to modular
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* Clock sub-menu: PPQN=24, Run=Y
* OUT 1: Gate /4
* OUT 2: Triangle /1

Ext DIN Sync:

- Ext DIN Sync (Clock) c> Pamela's NEW Workout (Clk)
- Ext DIN Sync (Run) g> Pamela's NEW Workout (Run) // slave sync
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Humanised hi-hat via Slop
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 1 Gate /16 EStep=16 ETrig=12 · Slop=15% · each hit drifts slightly in time · repetitive pattern stops feeling robotic · raise to 30% for drunk-drummer feel
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /16, EStep=16 ETrig=12, Slop=15%

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Hi-Hat (Trig) // humanised timing

patchIdeas.name: CV1 sweeps Euclidean rotation
patchIdeas.patch: External slow LFO → CV 1 input · Assign CV 1 to OUT 1 ERot parameter · attenuation set so full range covers 0-16 · slow sweep walks pattern through all 16 rotations · performance mutation without touching encoder
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /16, EStep=16 ETrig=5
* CV 1 → ERot (OUT 1), full range

LFO:

- LFO (Out) >> Pamela's NEW Workout (CV 1)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // CV sweeps through all rotations

patchIdeas.name: Bank advance via Run
patchIdeas.patch: Run input reconfigured to Load Sequenced Bank · slow clock output (OUT 8 /64) → Run · CV 1 low (forward direction) · every 64 beats the full 8-output setup advances to the next saved bank · automatic scene progression for long-form pieces
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's NEW Workout:
* Run mode: Load Sequenced Bank
* OUT 8: Gate /64
* Banks a, b, c, d saved

- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 8) g> Pamela's NEW Workout (Run) // self-advance banks
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- Pamela's NEW Workout (OUT 2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)
